IMPORTANT: BY PROVIDING PERSONAL DATA TO PHIVE AND/OR THROUGH THE PHIVE.PT WEBSITE, THE USER AUTHORIZES THE USE AND PROCESSING OF THEIR PERSONAL DATA BY PHIVE UNDER THE TERMS AND CONDITIONS DESCRIBED BELOW.

PHIVE undertakes to maintain the privacy and confidentiality of all information provided by the User. This Privacy Policy establishes the measures adopted by PHIVE regarding Personal Data collected directly from the User and/or through the PHIVE.PT website, in accordance with the Personal Data Protection Law and other applicable legislation, both at national and EU level. The term “Personal Data” refers to all personal information provided by the User, such as name, mobile phone number, profession, health information, date of birth, address, or email address.
11.2. Notification in the event of changes to the Privacy Policy

The constant effort to improve the means and add new features to the PHIVE.PT website, as well as to existing services, which translate into constant changes and improvements, changes to the applicable legal regime and the evolution of the technology itself, our practices may dictate changes with regard to data processing. When there is any change to this Privacy Policy, PHIVE will notify the User, as well as the local authority responsible, in cases required by law.
11.3. Collection of Personal Data including Email Address
Access to certain services on the PHIVE Website may require the User to provide the following information: name, address, email address, telephone number, and payment details.

You may also be asked to provide demographic information so that PHIVE can provide you with a more personalized service. The information provided will be stored in PHIVE's databases, either manually or electronically. PHIVE may supplement the information provided by the User with information provided by third parties. For example, if an incorrect postal code is provided, PHIVE may use third parties to correct this information.

PHIVE will provide the User with the necessary information so that they can give their prior consent to the processing of their personal data by PHIVE and its incorporation into the corresponding files owned by PHIVE. The data collected is processed automatically and is used, within the business group to which PHIVE belongs, to establish the status of User/Customer, for the subsequent collection of vouchers and future contacts, namely direct marketing actions, offers of products and ancillary services related, even indirectly, to PHIVE's activity. The data collected is also intended for compliance with legal obligations and for use in contractual relationships with insurance companies, banks, and other institutions. PHIVE undertakes to maintain strict confidentiality regarding all data, restricting access to it to what is strictly necessary for its normal activity. PHIVE may share, transfer, or disclose information from its database and server records in cases where it is legally required to do so, in the administration of justice, interacting with anti-fraud databases, to protect the vital interests of the User, to protect the security or integrity of our databases or website, to take action against legal liability, or in the event of the sale, merger, reorganization, dissolution, or other change at PHIVE.

The User authorizes the transmission of their data to PHIVE's commercial partners and also authorizes PHIVE to use the data for advertising, marketing, or promotional purposes, even after the membership contract has expired.

In the event of non-compliance by the User, the data may be transmitted to third parties for the purpose of debt collection. The respective owner is guaranteed, under the terms of Law 67/98 of October 26 (Personal Data Protection Law) and General Data Protection Regulation 2016/679, the right to access and rectify, delete or block, oppose, and transfer their data. The information will be sent by PHIVE to the address, email, or telephone number provided by the User, with the request being made within a maximum period of 30 days. The contacts provided will be kept in our database and may be used to occasionally send you information by email or post about products and services similar to those contracted by the User, which are considered to be of interest to them.

If the User does not wish to receive this type of information, they should proceed in accordance with the “Cancellation” section. In cases where the User has consented, their Personal Data may be shared with other companies belonging to the same group as PHIVE or disclosed to other companies that may contact the User about their products or services that may be of interest to them. PHIVE may also request and collect the User's Personal Data for, among other things, the following purposes:
Sending information about new products and services;
Regular updates that PHIVE considers to be of interest to the User;
Sending requested information about products and services;
For market research and marketing purposes;
For internal use in the administration of the PHIVE.PT website and improvement of the services provided.
a) PHIVE is required to notify the personal data security control entity, which is currently the National Data Protection Commission, of all data breaches that pose a risk to the data subject.
b) This notification must be made within 72 hours.

c) The notification must contain a description of the nature of the personal data breach, including the number of people concerned and the personal data records in question, the name and contact details of the data protection officer, a description of the likely consequences of the personal data breach, and a description of the measures proposed by the data controller to remedy the rights violated.

d) When the data breach is likely to cause harm to the data subject, namely when it may represent a high risk to their rights and freedoms, the data subject must be informed clearly and without delay.

To change your Personal Data, you must send your request in writing and indicate which data you wish to change. We will also correct and remove any Personal Data that is incorrect. Your data can always be updated using the contact details below.

If you no longer wish to receive emails from PHIVE and would like to be removed from the mailing list, please send an email to proteccaodados@phive.org with the subject line “Removal from Mailing List.” This cancellation will be effective within 30 days of the date of the request.
11.8. Collection of Anonymous Data through the PHIVE.PT Website

In addition to collecting data through the means indicated above, PHIVE uses technology that allows it to collect information about users of the PHIVE.PT website. For example, the website server automatically collects information about which pages are visited, the user's IP address, and which browser is used. This technology does not allow the user to be personally identified and is used for statistical purposes only. Our website contains hyperlinks to other pages on our website. Technology may also be used to monitor how often these links are used and which pages are most viewed. Again, this technology does not allow the User to be personally identified – it is used solely for statistical purposes.

To collect the information referred to in the previous paragraph, temporary cookies may be used, which will remain in the cookie file of the User's browser until it is closed. Cookies alone do not serve to identify the User. A cookie is a small piece of information sent to the User's server and stored on their computer's hard drive, causing no damage to it. You can set your browser to notify you whenever you receive a cookie, so that you can choose whether to accept or reject it. We also use your IP address to help diagnose problems with our server and to administer our website. An IP address is a numerical code that identifies a computer on the network, or in this case, on the Internet. Your IP address is also used to collect demographic information. We may also conduct IP searches to determine which domain you came from (e.g., aol.com, yourcompany.com) to better assess your demographic data.

If you are located in a country other than the one where PHIVE.PT's servers are located, communications may result in cross-border data transfers. By visiting our website and communicating with PHIVE electronically, you consent to the processing and transfer of your Personal Data as set out in this Privacy Policy.
PHIVE may deny access to previous guests even when they are people who participate in contests promoted by PHIVE or partners.

In the event of a consumer dispute, as defined in Law No. 144/2015 of September 8, the consumer may resort to the competent alternative dispute resolution entity.

Without prejudice to the provisions of the legislation, statutes, and regulations to which alternative consumer dispute resolution entities are bound, consumers may opt for the European online dispute resolution platform available at https://webgate.ec.europa.eu/odr, the alternative consumer dispute resolution entity in their place of residence, or the alternative dispute resolution entity with specialized competence, if one exists for the sector in question.

If there is no alternative dispute resolution entity under the terms of the previous paragraph, or if the existing entity or entities are not considered competent due to the value of the dispute, the consumer may resort to the National Center for Information and Arbitration of Consumer Conflicts, located in Lisbon, with the email address: cniacc@unl.pt and available at www.arbitragemdeconsumo.org.

In the event of a consumer dispute, consumers may resort to the European Online Dispute Resolution Platform, available at http://ec.europa.eu/consumers/odr, or to the following alternative dispute resolution entities:
Coimbra District Consumer Conflict Arbitration Center
